name: projects/project/locations/location/instances/instance/emailTemplates/18
content:
Email content 
Key Aspects of Jet Ski Testing
Water Testing (Performance Evaluation):
WOT Pull: Perform a Wide Open Throttle (WOT) test for 10-15 seconds to ensure the jet ski reaches maximum RPMs and top speed.
Handling: Assess the handling, especially if the hull has been treated with coatings like Super Slick 2000.
Electric Conversion: Test controls (throttle, steering), battery levels, and ensure the vessel is waterproof.
Acceleration and Vibration: Check for sluggish acceleration or unusual vibrations during operation.
Mechanical & Engine Testing:
Compression Test: Measure engine cylinder compression, ensuring they are within roughly 10 PSI of each other.
Pressure Testing (2-Strokes): Use a leakdown tester to pressurize the engine to 6-9 PSI for about 3 minutes to identify leaks in gaskets.
Electrical System: Check for starting issues, warning lights, and ensure the battery is charging properly with a multimeter.
